product_title: Facial Kit For Women - Includes Facial Mask, Facial Makeup Wipes, Nose Strips, Facial Cleansers, Facial- Self Care Package For Women (Gold Facial Kit)
clean_review_text: This "facial kit" has all the items you'd expect to get in a spa gift basket, only it's shipped in a cardboard box reminiscent of subscription boxes. The main difference is that it's a one-time deal, no subscription needed, and all the items are grouped together by theme and color. I ordered the "gold" theme and was a little disappointed that not all the items are gold colored (the eye mask is blue) and there's a mix of drugstore items with some more luxurious feeling ones. I'll start with the good. The box is very cute, with the words "It's a great day to love your face" greeting you when you open the box. Inside you get a five count box of sheet Masks that contain retinol, a generous and thick box of facial wipes, a Gold under-eye pad to reduce lines and puffiness, and a gold Lip Mask (I've never used a "lip mask" before so this seems a little bizarre, if fun). The blue eye mask is filled with gel beads so you can use it hot or cold, and the cheapest and least impressive items are the "facial buff" (a pear-shaped sponge that looks like something you'd clean your pots with) and a "facial cleansing brush" that looks a little cheap but is nevertheless useful. The main thing that was missing was some packing peanuts or confetti, as the items were tossed into the box hither and thither with NO padding, whereas other gift baskets and boxes typically include some stuffing so that the items are more artfully displayed. This one wasn't, so if I were giving this to someone as a gift, I'd certainly add it so the items wouldn't arrive in a jumble. But otherwise, I liked it.

product_title: KIMUSE Lash Enhancing Serum, Castor Oil, Eyelash Growth Serum and Eyebrow Enhancer, Promotes Appearance of Longer, Thicker Eyelashes, Fuller Thicker Lashes & Brows (5ML), Cruelty Free (lash enhancing serum)
clean_review_text: I’ve been trying out a couple of different lash serums over the past month. With my husband also using it (on his brows), while I use it on my lashes, we’ve been able to try different brands to see if there are any differences in results. This serum, by Kimuse, is very similar to the others in consistency (a thin liquid) and in how it’s applied (with a very thin brush). I have had no negative reactions to the serum, and no irritation to my eyes even when I’ve slipped and gotten some in my eye. This serum boasts results in as little as 3-4 weeks, while most others are more conservative at 6 weeks. In the few weeks we’ve been using the serums, neither of us have noticed any dramatic changes as far as lash or brow growth, but I do think I’m losing fewer lashes which is a definite plus. Honestly, I’m not sure if any serum is going to provide dramatic results, but we’ll continue for at least a few more weeks and see what happens while hoping for the best.Hopefully, I’ll be back with exciting news! Update: After using this, as well as other, lash growth serums for several months, I have noticed a definite improvement in the length of my lashes. I have not noticed as significant an improvement in the thickness, or volume, of my lashes, however. I believe that these types of serums do help promote growth, and I haven't been able to detect much difference between brands except that a couple of them irritated my eyes. Therefore, my conclusion is that this is helpful and the real determination of what works best will be what's most comfortable for you to use, so read the ingredients and watch for any possible allergy triggers or irritants.

clean_review_title: Nice smooth glide and large head makes for easy work
clean_review_text: The Maybuy Professional Hair Clippers/Beard Trimmers comes with 3 attachments, 1/2 inch, 1 inch and 1.5 inches. These attachments simply clip onto the head of the trimmer. You also get a small brush for cleaning the trimmer and a charging cable which strangely is a USB to Mini-USB even though at the bottom of the trimmer is your standard two prong charging connector. Being able to charge this via USB however is great for when you need to go on holiday/vacation as you always have some kind of USB charging device with yourself (usually for your phone). On a full charge the clippers will roughly 2 hours and are small enough to just throw in your toiletry bag. The trimmer itself has a nice shape to it and is comfortable to hold in your hand. It also has a fairly nice heft to it, not too light but not heavy. It feels like you are really holding on to something though. The head is fairly wide which can be a plus in that it doesn't take long at all to trim an area but can also be a bit of a minus depending on the shape of your face if you are trimming your beard. clean_review_title: Fantastic Jeans for Cold Weather- True To Size And COMFORTABLE
clean_review_text: FABRIC These jeans are made from fabric that is a nice soft denim with decent body to it, and very soft lightweight lining which resembles the faux fur type lining you will find in leather gloves. ZIPPER The zipper is about 6 inches long and has a locking lever which keeps it up while wearing the jeans. BUTTON The button is on securely so far and stays fastened when wearing. POCKETS The front pockets are deep enough to keep things from falling out, but they do not have a panel connected to the front zipper for slimming effect. The back pockets are small, but well placed and flattering. STYLE The style is really nice. Ankles are small enough to slide into boots, but large enough to be easy to get on and off. The rear seam in the back should be about an inch longer (I have made and designed jeans for years) but a belt keeps them up in the back okay. The front hits just below my navel. SIZING I take a size 16 misses US and this in size 16 fits perfectly and is comfortable. LINING I have some photos of the lining for you to see. It is lightweight but warm and very soft. It stretches with the pants. THE BOTTOM LINE These pants are fantastic to wear on chilly days or in places where you expect to be a bit cold. They are also great for wearing with boots in winter weather. MY RATING These are a FIVE STAR rating for me. I did not expect them to fit as pants usually give me trouble, but these are really nice and quite a surprise. I would definitely buy another pair if they ran as nice as these do size wise.

clean_review_text: I received this bathrobe directly from the manufacturer at no cost for evaluation, and here are my thoughts. This kimono style bathrobe is soft and substantial without being heavy. I'm 5'8, weigh approximately 142, and it fits me very comfortably. The robe is very well made with impeccable stitching and same color piping on the front and sleeves. It's thick enough to feel substantial, and feels comparable to the robes available at resorts. Woven of a modal, cotton, and polyester blend, the robe is a crisp white color, soft, very absorbent, and machine washable for easy care. It's kimono style design features comfortably wide cuffed sleeves, a sash closure with belt loops, and two front pockets. Other than a few loose thread ends that I snipped off, I didn't find any fault in the workmanship of this robe, and I didn't see any shrinkage after I washed it. It feels soft and smooth against my skin, and it's comfortable as well as absorbent. It's thick enough for good coverage an comfort in most seasons, but isn't the sort of robe you snuggle up with for warmth in winter. This is a full length robe that comes to just above my ankles, so while it fits me very nice (at 5'8"), a smaller person might find it a bit long. Overall this is a good looking, well made, and lightweight robe.

clean_review_title: DO NOT BUY THIS. WON'T FIT.
clean_review_text: If your 8 inch heating element has 4 circles and your 6 inch has 3 circles then these will not fit even when the item is listed as 8 inch and 6 inch. Look at the 8 and 6 inch in this listing. They each have one more circle. The legs for the 8 and 6 inch elements are also extended by 1/4 inch. Basically get and match exactly the amount of circles of your current heating elements or you'll be sorry you bought it. Also avoid every listing on Amazon or Ebay that doesn't match the amount of circles of your current heating elements.

clean_review_title: Never thought I needed one till I got one.
clean_review_text: Bought this back in July. I live in the south, and I do not have central air, so summer is basically a humid hellscape for a solid 3 months. I decided that I'd like to try an ice maker, grabbed this one, and have been pleasantly surprised by it. The ice is a bullet shaped, which took me a bit to get used to, but it works quick, and you get two choices of size/speed. Smaller is made faster, larger is slower. Make a batch, pop in a ziploc bag and lob in freezer, boom you have ice when you need it. I bought this one because I have very limited shelf space, and more importantly, it has a handle (as seen in the pic). My mom has a different make/model and has no handle, and she is jealous of my ability to easily move my ice machine around. It's a pretty great little machine. I'd have appreciated something telling me where to pour the water, since there's nothing showing you and if this is the first ice maker you have used, it's not really clear where it goes. Spoiler alert, pour it in the same side as the basket. It's fairly quiet. Not whisper quiet, and I wouldn't try to sleep next to it, but it hasn't disturbed me while I've been doing other stuff. All in all, it's a great little machine.

product_title: Keurig My K-Cup Reusable Coffee Filter - Old Model
clean_review_text: I've had my Keurig for 2 years, and my re-usable K-cup for 1 year. It works perfectly just as it is, as long as you don't overfill it and follow the directions. Oh, wait...mine didn't HAVE directions. When I first got it, I won't lie...I screwed it all up. The blasted thing didn't have directions, so I thought the cute little basket went right into the K-cup part of the Keurig. I had not a clue that the black K-cup holder part with the needle that pokes the bottom of the K-cup had to come OUT of my Keurig to put the gray part in. I made a huge mess. So, here are the directions for everyone else, so you don't have to be a dummy like me: 1. That place in your Keurig where K-cups normally go? Yeah, it actually comes out of the machine. Really, it does. There are three little tabs you have to push on the rim of it to pull it out. Get that thing out of there. It's not difficult, so if you're being rough with it, stop. It comes out pretty easily. Be careful you don't gouge yourself with the stabby-thingies in the machine. 2. Open up your reusable gray K-cup filter, wash it, and put about a tablespoon of coffee in there. If you like it strong like me, I suggest a finer grind. If you use Folgers, put it on a smaller cup setting or you won't get it very strong. This little bugger is made for just one cup at a time (whereas I can usually get one big cup or two little cups out of a disposable K-cup). 3. PUT THE LID BACK ON (!!!) the gray K-cup filter and put it into the spot where the regular black one usually lives (amazing, it fits!). Close it up and let 'er rip. 4. If you got coffee everywhere, you did something wrong. I know, I know...I didn't want to believe it either. Maybe you didn't put the lid on, or put the cute little basket into the machine without the gray part. Whatever. Either way, it's not the product's fault that you're dumb like me. :o) Start over, figure out what you messed up, and enjoy your coffee when you finally have that forehead-slap moment.

clean_review_title: New Viking 30" gas range a winner
clean_review_text: I had purchased a Wolf 30" gas range but the infrared broiler was not as good as my 16 year old Viking gas range. Wolf is a very reputable and reliable company and took back the range that I had for a few months and returned my money (a first class outfit). So I purchased a new Viking range and am thrilled with some of the new features. The self cleaning ones have been discontinued so this one is not self cleaning. The shelves in the oven roll out and one burner has more BTUs than the rest for big jobs. Also the infrared oven is excellent and the top is now sealed so liquid does not drip down into the guts of the oven and rust it out like the old one. The company is not as generous as Wolf and made me purchase a new set of knobs with the color of my choice (black) for better visibility and would not take back the silver ones as an exchange. The only annoying feature of all the new ranges is that they turn off when the oven gets very hot, even when you are using the IR broiler. I crack the door open to cool it to defeat this stupid feature. The IR broiler can be used with the convection fan and this is excellent on the Viking but not allowed on the Wolf.

clean_review_text: Absolutely love this! It's compact so it is great for travel. Let me start with how it works. Finely ground your favorite roast and dump them onto the provided round filters. Place the aroepress on a sturdy mug and pour hot water over the grounds. Now bear in mind for an 8oz drink, all is needed is 1 tablesoon of ground coffee and water poured to the 1-1 1/2 mark. Doesn't sound like much. It didn't sound like much to me. However we are not making drip coffee but espresso. A little goes a long way. Now, slowly press down on the plunger till it presses against the grounds. The idea is to fill the rest of your cup with either hot milk, cream, or water depending on what drink you are making. The manual that is provided in the box was very helpful with detailed instructions on how to make the common drinks. It should be shipped with a stirring stick, a scoop, a funnel, a stack of filters, and a carrying bag. Quite a package deal.

product_title: Upgraded WPW10546503 W10546503 Dishwasher Top Rack Adjuster Compatible with Kitchenaid Dishwasher Parts Upper Rack W10546503 dishwasher replacement parts For Whirlpool kenmore Dishwasher Rack(9 Packs)
clean_review_text: While the pieces appear expertly cast, and the problem parts from a stronger polymer than OEM they say, my trouble came on reinstalling the rack in the tracks, for which I suspect there could be a very thin excess of material in the casting of the 4 pins that hold the rack on the tracks. Since the polymer is hard as nails, it wasn't going to budge against the stainless steel tracks. I had removed the original, broken rack once to see what was going on, and without knowing what I was doing (from experience or video) got it out pretty easily, and back in easily (now realizing the slide with the back pins.) I strongly suggest you try putting your old rack back in a few times (if you still have back pins lol) to see how that works. (Yes there are locking tabs to remove it, you'll figure all this out.) Then I would take just these 2 new side T parts and try slipping them in and out - without the rack attached - and see how that works. Just as easy? (I did not know to do this...) Because attached to the rack (the replacement of parts is simple, as described in the video), trying to get the rack back in the track was a b.... And I think there may either be a slight excess on material on the pin flares, or something was not quite perpendicular and square (could have been my assembly but I don't think so.) Anyway, after multiple attempts (first one side would sort of cooperate but not the other, then vice versa, and sliding the rear pins back was Not easy) I finally got both rear pins to do what they needed, front pins dropped in and job completed. I would hope this job is not needed for at least another 5yrs like the OEM parts.

We have used it for over a month with no improvement and this is after dozens of gallons of water have been filtered through it. If I leave a glass of water sitting for a couple of hours, you can taste a distinctive glue-like taste that initially is a little like bitter lemon. I have gone back to bottle water and am filling the ice bin with store bought ice for now. I paid for this “better” filter to try and reduce contaminants for a member of our household who has immune problems. I am seriously concerned that using this filter is actually making this person sicker! I flushed and cleaned the entire refrigerator water system without the filter, and then reinstalled, but the taste is still there. I cannot recommend this filter. If I had not thrown out the original box, I would definitely return this item. Furthermore, I didn’t realize I was buying the filter from an appliance store; should have checked that it was direct from Whirlpool. Don't know if that contributed to the problem or not, but this filter is trash.

I ordered two sets of these filter baskets so that there would always be one ready for use. These filters work very well, are sturdy and well made.
